channel.title: Awesome Search KG
channel.description: Last 10 notes on Awesome Search KG

item.title: Doug Turnbull
item.description: Doug Turnbull Co-founder of OpenSource Connections (OSC); co-author of Relevant Search (O’Reilly).

item.title: Adrien Grand
item.description: Adrien Grand Principal engineer at Elastic, core Apache Lucene committer.

item.title: Joon-Pil (JP) Hwang
item.description: Joon-Pil (JP) Hwang Search engineer at Weaviate. Co-implemented Weaviate’s Block-Max WAND keyword search optimization (v1.29 technical preview).

item.title: André Mourão
item.description: André Mourão Search and IR engineer at Weaviate.

item.title: Block-Max WAND
item.description: Block-Max WAND Definition Block-Max WAND (BMW) is an enhancement of the WAND (Weak AND) algorithm that divides posting lists into fixed-size blocks, each with its own local maximum impact score.

item.title: BlockMax WAND: How Weaviate Achieved 10x Faster Keyword Search
item.description: BlockMax WAND: How Weaviate Achieved 10x Faster Keyword Search Authors: André Mourão, Joon-Pil (JP) Hwang (Weaviate) Published: 2025-02-26 Weaviate implemented Block-Max WAND as a technical preview in v1.29, achieving p50 query speedups of 5–10x and index size reductions of 50–90% over their existin...

item.title: Faster Retrieval of Top Hits in Elasticsearch with Block-Max WAND
item.description: Faster Retrieval of Top Hits in Elasticsearch with Block-Max WAND Author: Adrien Grand (Elastic) Published: 2019-02-05 Block-Max WAND was introduced into Lucene 8.0 and Elasticsearch 7.0 by Adrien Grand, delivering 3x–15x speedups for top-k retrieval.

item.title: Adopting Vespa for Recommendation Retrieval at Vinted
item.description: Adopting Vespa for Recommendation Retrieval at Vinted Vinted (Europe’s largest second-hand fashion marketplace) migrated from FAISS to Vespa for serving personalized homepage recommendations.

item.title: Agentic Search for Context Engineering
item.description: Agentic Search for Context Engineering Long-form write-up of the workshop given at AI Engineer Europe 2026 (London, April 8, 2026).

item.title: Beyond Semantic Similarity: Rethinking Retrieval for Agentic Search via Direct Corpus Interaction
item.description: Beyond Semantic Similarity: Rethinking Retrieval for Agentic Search via Direct Corpus Interaction Authors: TIGER-Lab (Zhuofeng Li et al.) Abstract Modern retrieval systems — whether lexical or semantic — expose a corpus through a fixed similarity interface compressing access into a single top-k retr...
